human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, public authority sources, scenario coverage, evaluation results, deployment package, and marketing notes against issue #124 and docs/agent-delivery-contract.md.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to enterprise policy and standards guidance, source traceability, exception criteria, and risk-based update prioritization.

Real public authoritative sources from NIST, ISO, COSO, ISACA, Microsoft, AWS, and Google Cloud are documented in the manifest with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy.

The local package defines policy-and-standards-specific scenarios, request and response contracts for shared meta-agents, and fallback behavior when delegated outputs are unavailable, stale, low-confidence, or boundary-conflicting.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffolding consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #124.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening the specialty boundary.
